page: Microsoft Azure L32s_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Lsv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, providing 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and includes a massive 7679 GB of local storage that is Premium Storage capable. No dedicated GPU hardware is included.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance in specific workloads. The server achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2147.0 and a multi-core score of 18442.0, both placing in the strong tier. Image processing is a notable highlight, registering in the top tier at 18415.0. Memory bandwidth for large block read-only operations is strong at 197.62 GB/sec, while write operations average 86.34 GB/sec. For compression tasks, single-threaded gzip compression performs strongly at 31.48 GB/sec, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches 209.27 GB/sec.

In language model inference tests, the server demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds of 392.66 tokens/sec for small 135M models, alongside strong prompt processing speeds of 2645.88 tokens/sec. For medium 7B models, prompt processing remains strong at 196.04 tokens/sec, though text generation drops to an average of 26.04 tokens/sec. Database operations and key-value transactions are stable, with Redis SET operations averaging 45.47 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring 17813.5.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the L32s_v4 offers high resource density by pairing a large 7679 GB local storage capacity with 256.0 GB of RAM, reducing the need for external storage attachments for data-heavy operations. This hardware profile is suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, static web serving, and memory-intensive analytical workloads that benefit from high-capacity local storage and dedicated Intel Xeon processing.
description: Microsoft Azure L32s_v4 is a storage and memory-optimized server from the Lsv4 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It delivers 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 7679 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 virtual machine excels in data-intensive tasks, showing strong performance in Geekbench multi-core tests (18442.0) and large-block memory read bandwidth (197.62 GB/sec). Benchmark data highlights top-tier capabilities in image processing and small-model LLM text generation. With its high local storage capacity and balanced memory-to-core ratio, the L32s_v4 is designed for workloads such as large databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L32s_v4 is an Intel Xeon-powered server featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7679 GB of local storage, delivering strong database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L32s_v4 is a 32-vCPU server with an Intel Xeon processor, 256 GB RAM, and 7679 GB of local storage for database workloads.
tagline: An Intel-powered virtual machine featuring high-capacity local storage and balanced memory density for data-intensive database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
7679 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong memory read bandwidth of 197.62 GB/sec for large blocks
Suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and static web serving
